test_output_summary: 34 passed in 0.05s. All tests pass. No regressions in existing test suites (150 pre-existing config tests still pass).
implementation_notes: Implemented config/logging.py with JsonFormatter class and configure_logging(config: LoggingConfig) function per ADR-ARCH-007. The JsonFormatter outputs single-line JSON with 'level' and 'message' fields, plus any extra fields merged from the 'extra' dict parameter. configure_logging() installs a StreamHandler with JsonFormatter on the root logger, clears existing handlers, and sets the root level from config.level. The implementation follows the existing pattern in synthesis/synthesise.py but is parameterised by LoggingConfig. Updated config/__init__.py to export JsonFormatter and configure_logging. Wrote 34 comprehensive tests covering all 4 acceptance criteria plus JsonFormatter unit tests and API-entrypoint.md progress logging format compatibility.
requirements_addressed: Support log levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR
Use Python's logging module with a JSON formatter
Set root logger level from config
Log format matches API-entrypoint.md progress logging examples

completion_promises.criterion_text: Structured JSON logging active after configure_logging() call
completion_promises.evidence: TestConfigureLoggingActivation verifies: root logger has exactly one handler with JsonFormatter after configure_logging(), previous handlers are replaced, and reconfiguration is idempotent. 4 tests cover this criterion.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Log level set from config (BDD: "Structured logging is configured from the config file")
completion_promises.evidence: TestLogLevelFromConfig parametrically verifies all 4 log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) are correctly set on the root logger from LoggingConfig.level. Additional tests verify DEBUG messages are emitted when level=DEBUG and INFO messages are suppressed when level=ERROR. 4 parametrised + 3 explicit = 7 tests.

completion_promises.criterion_text: JSON format output on all log lines
completion_promises.evidence: TestJsonFormatOutput verifies: every log line is valid JSON, contains 'level' and 'message' fields, extra fields are merged into top-level payload (matching API-entrypoint.md format), multiple log lines are each independently valid JSON, and all 4 log levels produce valid JSON. TestProgressLoggingFormat tests verify exact match with API-entrypoint.md examples (startup, progress, complete events). TestJsonFormatter unit tests verify single-line output and non-serialisable fallback. 13+ tests cover this criterion.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Works with Python standard logging module
completion_promises.evidence: TestStandardLoggingModuleIntegration verifies: configure_logging uses the root logger, child loggers inherit JSON format, the standard logging.getLogger(__name__) pattern works, and %-style string formatting in log messages produces correct JSON output. 4 tests cover this criterion.
